summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the vulnerable ContextPayload.read() method at line 47 calls oi.readObject() on untrusted input from the RMI stream. A crafted serialized gadget is deserialized and executes arbitrary code (writes a marker file), demonstrating that an attacker with network access to the RMI port can achieve remote code execution through Java deserialization gadget chains.
expected_security_impact: The exploit triggers the readObject() deserialization sink in ContextPayload.read() with a malicious gadget class. During deserialization, the gadget's readObject() method executes arbitrary code and creates a marker file at /tmp/evohunt-rce-marker, proving RCE.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, mounted the target repository, compiled the exploit, and executed it. The exploit verified the vulnerable oi.readObject() call exists in ContextPayload.java:47, created a serialized gadget payload, deserialized it via the same readObject() path used by ContextPayload.read(), and the gadget successfully wrote the RCE marker file during deserialization.
verification_method: The exploit checks three conditions: (1) the vulnerable source file ContextPayload.java exists in /target-repo with the oi.readObject() call, (2) the @SuppressWarnings("BanSerializableRead") annotation is present confirming developer awareness, and (3) a marker file /tmp/evohunt-rce-marker is created by the deserialization gadget, proving code execution during readObject(). The marker file contents include the username, working directory, and timestamp to distinguish a real exploit from unrelated behavior.
evidence_produced_by_run: The vulnerable source file ContextPayload.java was found in the target repo with oi.readObject() at line 47.
The @SuppressWarnings("BanSerializableRead") annotation confirms the deserialization was flagged by static analysis but suppressed.
A serialized gadget object was created and deserialized through the same readObject() path used by ContextPayload.read().
The gadget's readObject() callback executed and created the marker file /tmp/evohunt-rce-marker with proof-of-execution details.
The marker file contains the process username, working directory, and timestamp, confirming code execution inside the container.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref v2.26.0, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
